ab: akalrolaili dha’anenai omiki ’oponoho, asolasolatengali mani, ’aelanai moa hiito, mahadhaolonga miki lrevake siikace.
ch: 小時候我們家住在萬山，上學以後，就搬到了屏東，一直在都市裡生活。
en: When we were young, our family lived in ’oponoho. After I started school, we moved to Pingtung and have been living in the city ever since.

ab: aoangali dhaigako, mani pilrilrao ta kaeaea kaocae bongka ’i solate, ta’asalrao lrio’o taiwan kaocae ta tohamoho ma’akaelela tai’aeni bongka.
ch: 上了大學以後，我修了一堂關於原住民文化的課程，瞭解到臺灣原住民族有各式各樣豐富的文化。
en: After I went to college, I took a course on aboriginal culture and learned that the aboriginal people in Taiwan have various and rich cultures.

ab: to’atolohaeli, ’ina mene caili kalalrapa’ae taivalrivalriyae, ilraae la takali ’aolai kaava’inai movalriyo ’itovolrovolro ta kaahodhange, nai lriho’o toce’elao zokonai bongka iyanai.
ch: 因此，今年暑假時，我和哥哥回到部落訪問老人家，想要瞭解自己族群的文化。
en: So this summer, my brother and I went back to our tribe to visit the elderly, hoping to understand the culture of our own ethnic group.

ab: ta kahodhange otahamavalrolonga caili, lalo cengelemaidhe i’ana ta icengecenge make’ecenge ocaodha.
ch: 老人家已經八十歲了，但是看起來還像中年人。
en: The old man is already eighty years old, but he still looks like a middle-aged man.

ab: mani ’itovolrolraidhe kavahadha alriho’ae’o, alriho’aedha macaci’o ’a ’oponoho vaha, la lriiho’o paiwan vaha, bonong vaha la nipong vaha.
ch: 我問他會哪些語言，他的萬山語說得很流利，也會說排灣語、布農族話和日本話。
en: I asked him which languages he knew, and he was fluent in ’oponoho, as well as Paiwan, Bunun and Japanese.

ab: a’itovolrongalidhe ta’asalrao lriho’o, akalrolaidha ’a ’ipato’oto’onga nipong vaha, la lamengaedha ’a tali paiwan ocao, la ’ihakii taikiedha ’asika’olo mani palrihilriho’o tali bongo ocao, to’atolohaedha lriho’o kapa vahanga.
ch: 我問他才知道，原來他小時候就學日本話，太太是排灣族人，而他工作上認識許多布農族人，所以他會說這麼多語言。
en: I asked him to find out that he learned Japanese when he was a child, his wife is an Paiwan, and he knows many Bunun at work, so he can speak so many languages.

ab: ona a’itovolronaidhe, dhona ta kahodhange pato’iname ta kadhao ’aamadhalae tapapi’aeni ta alopo.
ch: 我們訪談的時候，老人家談論了許多關於狩獵的傳統知識。
en: During our interview, the old man talked about a lot of traditional knowledge about hunting.

ab: topodhaolae ka poelrekanai lriho’o sialalra, oha’eve la to’ahakinai soci lo’ing, dholromao ’oelaanga toce’elao siyalara.
ch: 雖然我們沒有辦法完全聽懂，但是我們有用手機錄影，可以再仔細聆聽。
en: Although we could’t fully understand it, we could record it on our cell phones and listen to it more.

ab: akalrolaili matilriho’okali ka zokonai bongkani, ilraae kaocaelrao lo miyali ’akoka’iniyae.
ch: 小時候我不瞭解自己族群的文化，對於自己是原住民有點不好意思。
en: When I was young, I didn’t understand the culture of my ethnic group, and I was a little embarrassed that I was an aborigine.

ab: alriho’ongali kinatodhaoenga, mani ’inyaiyangalrao ’ina ’oponoholra ocao, ’a maiconganae tai’aeni iyalrao, ’initatoelaelangalrao.
ch: 瞭解愈多以後，愈覺得自己身為萬山族人，是很獨特的，也很值得驕傲。
en: The more I know, the more I feel that being a ’oponoho is unique and something to be proud of.
